observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the generally accepted accounting principles on the reporting and accounting for collections, so as not to mislead the readers of the financial statements, pursuant to Sections 111(2), 112, 123 and 124 of P.D. No. 1445.  Henceforth, ensure proper reporting and accounting of collections based on the actual receipt of cash from the payee/taxpayer/s during the calendar year.

We further recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw a journal entry voucher to take-up the bookkeeping error in the accounting for collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the DVs totaling ₱8,024,098.08, together with their supporting documents, for review and audit purposes. 

Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting journal entry to record the stale checks totaling ₱11,120.00 as of December 31, 2023.

Moreover, we reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of stale checks for its cancellation and replacement, and to observe the timely submission of the DVS and their supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the : 

Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of inventory count especially to infrastructure assets; and, review and revise the ICF and IIRUP to identify correctly of which items are to be included in the ICF and IIRUP;

Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to trace and investigate the discrepancy of 153,057,873.09 between the RPCPPE and Accounting records; and

Municipal Engineer, Inventory Committee and the Property Custodian to locate, trace and provide records for the infrastructure assets of the LGU.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee has not yet completed the One-Time Cleansing of Property, Plant and Equipment

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the PPAs’ Focal Persons to review the financial records, statements, and prior years’ transactions in order to track the history and origin of the dormant fund transfers totaling ₱212,911.76; and

Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately return the balances of fund transfers for completed PPAs totaling ₱97,907.60.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to require the contractors to submit the As-Built Plan of completed infrastructure projects totaling ₱25,654,926.59 and immediately submit to the Audit Team for audit and verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

  Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year at the beginning of every year.

Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their respective records pertaining to balances of RPT/SET receivables.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed:

Require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:

Prepare and post the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of Real Property Tax at the main entrance of the municipal hall, and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each Barangay of the City, and publish it once a week for two (2) consecutive weeks, in a newspaper of general circulation in the municipality;

Prepare and issue the Notices of Real Property Tax Delinquency (first, second and final) to delinquent tax payers;

Avail remedies for collections of delinquent RPT and SET by administrative action through a levy on real property or by judicial action; and

Collect in full the computed penalties for collections of delinquent RPT/SET dues.

Xxx

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
 
Require the MT to:
 
Xxx

Require all revenue collectors/AOs to turn over the full amount of their collections intact at the close of business day to the LO; and

Xxx
observations.reason: Still, the RCCs do not remit daily the collections to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the: 

Xxx

Property Custodian-Designate and other offices concerned to comply with the provisions of the GAM for LGUs on recording of receipts and issuances of inventories, the maintenance of stock cards, and the use of RIS for issuances and the preparation of monthly RSMIs based on RISs and the submission of copies thereof to the Municipal Accountant for recording; and

Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories on a semestral basis, reconcile the result of physical count with the property and the accounting records, and prepare and submit a copy of the RPCI to the Audit Team within the reglementary period, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:  

record daily collections and deposits based on the RCDs; 

balance all her cashbooks daily; and 

Xxx
observations.reason: Still, daily collections were not recorded and balance her cashbooks daily

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Government of Tarangnan provide immediately the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with a safe or vault for the safekeeping of accountable forms and undeposited collections and/or undisbursed cash advance.  

Likewise, provide the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with a separate office space which is adequately protected from intrusions from unauthorized persons.
observations.reason:
Still, the MT was not provided with proper vault or safe

Still, the office space of the MT is open to other personnel

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management secure a comprehensive fire insurance for its properties with insurable interest so that the Municipality will be indemnified in case of loss or damage to its properties caused by natural and man-made calamities. 

Likewise, include in its budget the corresponding insurance premiums.
observations.reason: Comprehensive Insurance were not secured and still unfunded

observations.recommendation:
It is strongly recommended that the management should exert extra effort to trace its historical cost to its recorded valuation of Land, Land Improvements and Buildings and be reconciled with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor. 


Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipality should be supported either with an original or transfer certificates of title registered in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: The Municipality has started the process of titling for 3 parcels of land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive requires the Property Officer to prepare and submit SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to MA to support the recognition of appropriate expenditure account of all materials and supplies consumed or issued. The SSMI should be prepared on a monthly basis to cover the issuance of inventory items for the month to avoid accumulation of unrecorded issuance.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to update the CIP account as of year-end by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the CY 2024 CIP balance by ₱2,546,092.15, for the three (3) completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the inventory and expense accounts amounting to ₱448,000.00 to appropriate accounts.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal DRRM Officer to:
refrain from utilizing the fund for non-disaster risk reduction and management related programs, projects and activities; and
deliver immediately to the recipients the procured Evacuation Guides and Early Warning Signages.
Municipal Accountant and Municipal DRRM Officer to ensure that all LDRRMF disbursements are supported with complete supporting documents.

We recommended, and the management agreed that GAD Focal Points System ensure that the costs reflected in the GAD Plan and Budget for projects, programs and activities are accurately determined based on the level of GAD responsiveness of each PPA, as assessed using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tools in compliance with the minimum GAD appropriation requirement under RA No. 9710.

We recommended, and the management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Engineering Office to assess the cause and extent of the noted deterioration and recommend appropriate corrective measures; and
Require the contractor to implement the corrective measures under the projects’ warranty provisions if the deterioration was due to poor workmanship or substandard materials.

We recommended, and the management agreed to:
ensure preparation and issuance of approved Variation Order for all project modifications, regardless of cost implications; and
submit to the Audit Team Commission on Audit (COA) the approved Variation Order and supporting documents for technical review and inspection.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Local School Board:
Furnish copy of the Annual and Quarterly SEF utilization report to the Municipality’s Sangguniang Bayan and the DBM; and
Identify and utilize additional conspicuous public areas within the municipality for the posting of SEF utilization reports to ensure wider dissemination and enhance public transparency and awareness.
We also recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare and submit a copy of the Annual and Quarterly SEF utilization Reports to the LSB.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Local School Board develop a three-year program outlining strategic prioritization policies for allocating the SEF among schools within the municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit the following certified statement to the LSB not later than July 15 of the current year, to wit
Actual SEF Income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
Actual SEF Income for the first two quarters of the current year;
Estimated SEF Income for the last two quarters of the current year; and
Annual Estimates for the ensuing year
We further recommended that the LSB review and approve the SEF Budget for the ensuing year no later than October 31 of the current year to ensure timely and effective planning and budgeting of the SEF.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to include the related party disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements by including the total remuneration received by key management personnel (KMP) and the number of individuals in each KMP category, as well as the remuneration and compensation of their close family members, in compliance with IPSAS 20.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the four (4) DVs for disbursements totaling ₱942,261.05, together with the corresponding supporting documents Summary of Audit Suspensions, Disallowances, and Charges .

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting journal entry to correct the overstatement of the reported Repairs and Maintenance – Infrastructure Assets account was overstated by ₱9,193,785.00 and understatement of the Environment/ Sanitary Services, Other General Services and Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses by ₱7,096,570.00, ₱1,515,215.00 and ₱1,515,215.00, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to return the unspent balance of the Capital Outlay amounting to ₱8,524.86 to General Fund. 

direct the Municipal Budget Officer and other personnel concerned to avoid unnecessary inclusion of programs, projects and activities which are not related to the DRRM activities.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct, require and capacitate the:

HRMO to require the JO/COS workers to submit a copy of TIN before hiring and renewal of contracts;

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to compute, withhold and remit the respective percentage taxes of JO/COS workers for CY 2023 and the years thereafter (if in case they did not avail of the 8% income tax rate), as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations; and

Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to attend seminars and training related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR laws, rules and regulations.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE:

Facilitate the disposal of properties which were already returned to the PC and reported as unserviceable.  And the Municipal Accounting Office should prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of each PPE account.

We further recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and ensure that all future acquisitions of properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 will be recorded in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that the Management fully implement all eligible projects to be financed by the 20% development fund within the budget year, thru the coordinated efforts of the LGU officials, to achieve optimum desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes for its constituents.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to adhere
strictly to the generally accepted accounting principles on the reporting and
accounting for collections, so as not to mislead the readers of the financial
statements, pursuant to Sections 111(2), 112, 123 and 124 of P.D. No. 1445.
Henceforth, ensure proper reporting and accounting of collections based on
the actual receipt of cash from the payee/taxpayer/s during the calendar year.

We further recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw a
journal entry voucher to take-up the bookkeeping error in the accounting for
collections.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require the:

a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to
the Audit Team the DVs totaling ₱8,024,098.08, together with their
supporting documents, for review and audit purposes.

b. Municipal Accountant toprepare an adjusting journal entry to record the
stale checks totaling ₱11,120.00 as of December 31, 2023.

Moreover, we reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that that the
Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to
the payees of stale checks for its cancellation and replacement, and to observe
the timely submission of the DVS and their supporting documents.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require the :
a. Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of inventory count especially
to infrastructure assets; and, review and revise the ICF and IIRUP to
identify correctly of which items are to be included in the ICF and IIRUP;
b. Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to trace and
investigate the discrepancy of 153,057,873.09 between the RPCPPE and
Accounting records; and
c. Municipal Engineer, Inventory Committee and the Property Custodian to
locate, trace and provide records for the infrastructure assets of the LGU.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require the:
1. Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the PPAs’ Focal
Persons to review the financial records, statements, and prior years’
transactions in order to track the history and origin of the dormant fund
transfers totaling ₱212,911.76; and
2. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately
return the balances of fund transfers for completed PPAs totaling
₱97,907.60.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting journal entry to
correct the overstatement of the reported Repairs and Maintenance –
Infrastructure Assets account was overstated by ₱9,193,785.00 and
understatement of the Environment/ Sanitary Services, Other General
Services and Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses by ₱7,096,570.00,
₱1,515,215.00 and ₱1,515,215.00, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to return the
unspent balance of the Capital Outlay amounting to ₱8,524.86 to General
Fund.
b) direct the Municipal Budget Officer and other personnel concerned to
avoid unnecessary inclusion of programs, projects and activities which are
not related to the DRRM activities.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
instruct, require and capacitate the:
a. HRMO to require the JO/COS workers to submit a copy of TIN before
hiring and renewal of contracts;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to compute, withhold
and remit the respective percentage taxes of JO/COS workers for CY
2023 and the years thereafter (if in case they did not avail of the 8%
income tax rate), as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent
laws, rules and regulations; and
c. Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to attend seminars
and training related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR laws, rules
and regulations.

We recommended. and the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant,
agreed to require the contractors to submit the As-Built Plan of completed
infrastructure projects totaling ₱25,654,926.59 and immediately submit to the
Audit Team for audit and verification.

1.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees
regarding the existence of stale checks for its cancellation and replacement;
and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to Investigate the abovementioned
reconciling items and cause the preparation of the adjusting journal entry.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant of
a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year at the beginning of every year.
b. Municipal Accountant to adjust the CY 2022 balances for the
overstatement of RPT receivable and understatement of SET receivable by
₱613,473.72 and ₱353,689.68, respectively, to correct the established
amount for the year.
c. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their respective
records pertaining to balances of RPT/SET receivables.

3.27 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE:
a. Require the PC to attach, and replace the semi-detached and dilapidated
with the required property stickers for PPE with the prescribed number
format under COA Circular No. 2020-06 in order to have a uniform
property identification system; and indicate the value of each item in the
Inventory List of Property, Plant and Equipment.
b. Create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all their
regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or
less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to
the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled
start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full implementation of
"One-time cleansing of PPE accounts" as provided for in COA Circular
No. 2020-006 in order to address the unlocated and unreconciled PPEs.
c. Facilitate the disposal of properties which were already returned to the PC
and reported as unserviceable. And the Municipal Accounting Office
should prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance
of each PPE account.
3.28 We further recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Provide the correct the residual value of depreciable PPEs and compute
the monthly depreciation expense as provided under the current COA
issued accounting policies:
b. Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to Reclassify the PPEs amounting to
₱5,015,355.00 to appropriate accounts.
c. Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the properties below the
capitalization threshold and ensure that all future acquisitions of
properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 will be
recorded in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006.

4.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The concerned MHO personnel to submit immediately to the audit
team, thru the Accountant, the following documents to establish their
claim to OCA:
i. Office Order or equivalent from the LCE or his authorized
representative requiring the personnel (regular, contractual, or on
a job order) to report for work to the health facilities involved in
the COVID-19 response, especially the BHWs and personnel from
other departments;
ii. Daily Time Records with Biometric printouts or copies of logbook
or Certificate of Attendance in the health facilities involved in
COVID-19 response by their immediate supervisor during the
period of claims;
iii. Certification by the Municipal Health Officer, or his/her authorized
representative of the number of hours that the HCWs and non-
HCWs physically report for work in a month;
iv. detailed computation of OCA prepared by the LGU, which was
validated by the DOH-CHD and noted by the PHO Officer; and
v. Memorandum of Agreement and Resolution authorizing the LCE
to enter into a MOA with the DOH.
b. The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the payments of OCA
amounting to ₱699,750.00 from Other Bonuses and Allowances to
Other Personnel Benefits (regular employees) and Other Maintenance
and Operating Expenses (contractual employees) in the CY 2022
Statement of Financial Performance.

5.16 We recommended that the Management:
a. Allocate funds for Disaster Recovery and Rehabilitation areas as this is
also important in disaster and risk management;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose LDRRM Fund in the Notes
to Financial Statements using the format in Annex D of COA Circular No.
2012-001 for fair presentation and to enhance the understandability of the
users of financial statements.
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer to the Trust
Liability – DRRM account the ₱1,297,550.00 discrepancy of the amount
previously transferred.

6.5 We recommended that the Management fully implement all eligible projects
to be financed by the 20% development fund within the budget year, thru the
coordinated efforts of the LGU officials, to achieve optimum desirable socio-
economic development and environment outcomes for its constituents.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor assign additional staff in the
Municipal Assessor’s Office in order to address the under-staffing problem.
7.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Assessor update yearly the
assessment roll by conducting physical survey of all real properties and submit
it to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of
December each year.

8.23 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:
i. Prepare and post the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of Real
Property Tax at the main entrance of the municipal hall, and in a
publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each Barangay of the City,
and publish it once a week for two (2) consecutive weeks, in a
newspaper of general circulation in the municipality;
ii. Prepare and issue the Notices of Real Property Tax Delinquency (first,
second and final) to delinquent tax payers;
iii. Avail remedies for collections of delinquent RPT and SET by
administrative action through a levy on real property or by judicial
action; and
iv. Collect in full the computed penalties for collections of delinquent
RPT/SET dues.
b. Require the Municipal Government officials and employees to make
prompt payment of their real property tax dues.
c. Submit the Legal Basis on the reduction of tax assessments.

9.18 We recommended that the LCE direct the City Human Resource Management
Officer to:
a) Prepare the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan in
order to assess the required number of JO workers to be hired based
on necessity and financial capability of the Municipality;
b) Consider filling up vacant positions under Plantilla to minimize the
number of JO workers to be hired;
c) Ensure that Job Order contracts or Contracts of Service are clear and
unambiguous, duly signed by the JO workers, contain detailed duties
and responsibilities, compensation payment on a daily basis, specific
place of assignment, especially those assigned in field work, names are
consistently stated, signed by the witnesses, and are submitted to the
HRMO;
d) Ensure that the payroll is signed by the JO workers upon receipt of
wages, and ascertain that an SPA is presented by a person authorized
by the JO worker to claim wages; and
e) Adopt a monitoring mechanism in order to evaluate whether a JO
worker is qualified for the renewal of contract.

10.12 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Assign the receipt, safekeeping and transfer to PHO of the used and
discarded vials and other COVID-19 vaccination wastes functions to
Sanitation Inspector (SI);
b. Require the SI to properly store and transfer the vials and other COVID-
19 vaccination wastes together with the consolidated Form A – Reverse
Logistic, supported with DENR Manifest Form to PHO every Friday of
the vaccination week, and ensure that vaccine vials and other vaccination
wastes are placed in a yellow bag / plastic sack or any color of sack tied
with yellow ribbon labeled properly;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that received, issued and
balance of COVID-19 vaccines are disclose in the Notes Financial
Statements; and
d. Require the Property Custodian to:
i. Use the Acknowledge Receipt for In-Kind Donations for DRRM and
Issuance form for In-Kind Donations-DRRM for the receipt and
issuance of COVID-19 vaccines;
ii. Prepare and maintain the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for
DRRM and Inventory Report-DRRM for the receipt and utilization
of COVID-19 Vaccines;
iii. Prepare and submit to Office of the Civil Defense (OCD), Auditor of
the OCD and to COA Auditor, the Monthly Report on the Receipt
and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods for the received
COVID-19 vaccines;
iv. Prepare and submit the monthly Inventory List of Items for DRRM
supported with the required documents to COA and to Municipal
Accounting Office.

1.16 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
3. Require the MT to:
d) Prevent unauthorized transfer of accountable forms between AOs;
e) Require all revenue collectors/AOs to turn over the full amount of their
collections intact at the close of business day to the LO; and
f) Strictly verify the RCDs of AOs.
4. Increase the amount of fidelity bond to an amount sufficient to cover the
maximum amount of accountability in accordance with the 2019 Revised
Schedule of Premium Rates provided by the Bureau of Treasury.
1.17 We further recommended that the MA be required to draw an adjusting
journal entry to correct the understatement of Cash on Hand and income
accounts by ₱544,601.96 as of year end.

2.17 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
1. Require the PC to:
a. Attach the required property stickers for PPE with the prescribed
number format under COA Circular No. 2020-06 in order to have a
uniform property identification system;
b. Indicate the value of each item in the Inventory List of Property, Plant
and Equipment.
2. Create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all their
regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or
less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to
the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled
start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full implementation of
"One-time cleansing of PPE accounts" as provided for in COA Circular
No. 2020-006 in order to address the unlocated and unreconciled PPEs.
2.18 We further recommended that Management facilitate the disposal of
properties which were already returned to the PC and reported as
unserviceable. On the other hand, the Municipal Accounting Office should
prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of each
PPE account.

3.18 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
1. The concerned MHO personnel to submit immediately to the audit
team, thru the Accountant, the following documents to establish their
claim to SRAs under AO Nos. 36 and 42:
a. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance/ office order/
authority to report for work;
b. Daily Time Records with Biometric printouts/copies of logbook or
Certificate of Attendance in the COVID Facility/Center by their
immediate supervisor during the period of claims;
c. Accomplishment reports indicating the number of COVID patients
attended to in a day.
d. For health workers not directly catering COVID-19 patients, an
explanation as to the scientific basis for the premise that healthcare
workers not directly catering to COVID-19 patients are equally
exposed to COVID-19 and can be considered “in contact with
COVID-19 patients”, and thus should be given the SRA.
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the payments of SRA
to permanent and contractual employees amounting to ₱865,909.05 19
from Hazard Pay to Other Personnel Benefits (regular employees) and
Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses (contractual employees)
in the CY 2021 Statement of Financial Performance.

4.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to transfer
the current year unexpended balance of the QRF and MOOE amounting
to ₱3,462,773.74 to a Special Trust Fund. Henceforth, immediately transfer
the unexpended QRF and MOOE of LDRRMF from General Fund to
Trust Fund at every year end.
b) Refrain from appropriating, incurring and charging expenses not related
to disaster risk reduction as provided in the COA Circular No. 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012.

5.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed, to:
a. Cause the timely implementation of the development projects by the
Municipal Development Council through proper coordination with the
Municipal Engineer, the BAC and other Offices involved; and
b. Conduct thorough evaluation on the feasibility of proposed development
projects prior to identification and inclusion in the Annual/Supplemental
Investment Plan to preclude delay in project implementation and to
ensure optimal utilization of the 20% DF for the attainment of the
desirable socio-economic development of the Municipality.

6.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed, to require the Municipal Accountant
and other officials concerned to immediately submit the disbursement
vouchers totaling ₱113,009.18, together with their supporting documents, and
cancelled check.
6.7 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure that all disbursement vouchers
and their supporting documents, copies of cancelled checks and other
transaction documents are submitted on a regular and timely basis.

7.9 We recommended and Management agreed, that the Local Chief Executive
require the BAC through its Secretariat, to ensure that the contractor’s
submitted OSS contain the mandatory provisions stated in the GPPB Circular
04-2020 before awarding a contract.

8.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed, to submit at the beginning of the year
a list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs)
for the current year, and inform the COA within ten (10) days after the award
of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity, that
the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted as
required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others concerned to implement, without any further delay, the programs/projects under the 20% Development Fund which have not yet been started.  Henceforth, ensure that the 20% Development Fund is properly, promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:  

1) record daily collections and deposits based on the RCDs; 

2) balance all her cashbooks daily; and 

3) maintain a cashbook for Cash Advance under the Trust Fund, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM).

We recommended that the Municipal Government of Tarangnan provide immediately the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with a safe or vault for the safekeeping of accountable forms and undeposited collections and/or undisbursed cash advance.  

Likewise, provide the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with a separate office space which is adequately protected from intrusions from unauthorized persons.

We recommended that management consider the immediate closure or eventual phase out of the open dump site, and ensure that the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan be fully implemented pursuant to R.A. No. 9003 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain and update property cards per category of PPE and the Municipal Accountant to also maintain and update PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) for land.  Both officers should reconcile their records regularly.  Likewise, cause the conduct of physical inventory of all its properties and submit a report thereon.

We recommended that management secure a comprehensive fire insurance for its properties with insurable interest so that the Municipality will be indemnified in case of loss or damage to its properties caused by natural and man-made calamities. 

Likewise, include in its budget the corresponding insurance premiums.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require collecting clerks to turnover their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer and for the latter to remit her collections daily and intact to the depository bank. 

Likewise, require collecting clerks to prepare a Report of Collection and Deposits daily to facilitate turnover of collections.

It is strongly recommended that the management should exert extra effort to trace its historical cost to its recorded valuation of Land, Land Improvements and Buildings and be reconciled with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor. 

Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipality should be supported either with an original or transfer certificates of title registered in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: The Municipality has started the process of titling for 3 parcels of land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the deficiency, and henceforth, record transactions pertaining to the grant of cash advances to accountable officers and employees for special purpose/time-bound undertakings properly, pursuant to Section 112 of PD No. 1445 and the pertinent provisions of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
a)	Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain SLCs for Inventories Held for Distribution and other inventory items; to coordinate with the Property Custodian-Designate and other offices concerned to ascertain the accuracy and existence of the inventory account balances; and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries;
b)	Property Custodian-Designate and other offices concerned to comply with the provisions of the GAM for LGUs on recording of receipts and issuances of inventories, the maintenance of stock cards, and the use of RIS for issuances and the preparation of monthly RSMIs based on RISs and the submission of copies thereof to the Municipal Accountant for recording; and
Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories on a semestral basis, reconcile the result of physical count with the property and the accounting records, and prepare and submit a copy of the RPCI to the Audit Team within the reglementary period, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the variance in the reported PPE in the Statement of Financial Position and in the Schedule of Depreciation; and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the misstatements due to the provision of salvage value for Public Infrastructure Assets and the inclusion of semi-expendable items that failed to meet the criteria for capitalization threshold;
the Municipal Assessor and the MPDC to act on the processing of the TCTs of the parcels of land reported under the name of the Municipality, and to register them with the Register of Deeds without any further delay; and
the Property Custodian-Designate to prepare RPCPPE using the format prescribed in the GAM for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the LCE direct the submission of relevant and necessary documents to support the validity and propriety of the payment of COVID-19 Hazard Pay to the recipients/beneficiaries.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice(s) of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE to:
require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC, to submit the lacking documents as listed in Annex A, and, henceforth, ensure that  all financial transactions are supported with complete documentation, pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and
require the BAC to conduct continuous posting of the NOAs/contracts/POs of awarded projects, pursuant to Items 3.6 and 9 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, for reporting and monitoring purposes of fund utilization, in relation to the existing COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 19-2020, RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR, and other related regulations applicable during the time of procurement of Goods, Infrastructure Projects and Consulting Services.

We recommended that the LCE create an Inventory Committee, cause the conduct of a complete physical count of all properties and unissued inventories of the Municipality, and the subsequent preparation and submission of the RPCPPE and RPCI, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian-Designate reconcile the accounting and property records with the RPCPPE and RPCI.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to correctly withhold the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations of municipal officials and employees from their respective salaries, and remit them promptly to GSIS, Pag-IBIG Fund and PhilHealth, as mandated by RA No. 8291, RA No. 9679, and RA No. 7875 and their IRRs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to correctly withhold the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations of municipal officials and employees from their respective salaries, and remit them promptly to GSIS, Pag-IBIG Fund and PhilHealth, as mandated by RA No. 8291, RA No. 9679, and RA No. 7875 and their IRRs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to reclassify the fully depreciated properties from Other Property, Plant and Equipment account to the proper Property, Plant and Equipment accounts, and disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the LCE be more judicious in authorizing employees to attend seminars/trainings.  In choosing whom to send to a particular training, the LCE should consider the employee’s position/function, qualifications and ability to impart knowledge acquired from the training, among others.  

We also recommended that the Municipality refrain from conducting seminar-workshops in venues outside of the geographical island where the Municipality is located, particularly in expensive/luxury hotels, unless it can be shown that the choice of venue is most advantageous to the government.  Moreover, management should exercise prudence in utilizing the limited funds of the Municipality to avoid illegal, irregular, unnecessary, excessive, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-59.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the correct amount of taxes from all honoraria payments and remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) within the reglementary period, pursuant to Sections 5 and 7 of RR No. 11-2018.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive file a request for write-off of the Due from Local Government Units account, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others concerned to implement, without any further delay, all the programs/projects under the 20% Development Fund.  

We also recommended that henceforth, management ensure that the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities, where appropriate.  This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice/s of Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors concerned to post the required warranty securities. 

Henceforth, the management should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in the implementation of infrastructure projects and procurement of goods, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

We recommended that management desist from charging to government funds the holding of the Search for Mutya Han Tarangnan 2018 and strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ascertain the exact amount of share of each barangay from the Municipality’s Real Property Tax collections for CYs 2015-2018, and release them to the barangays immediately.  

Likewise, release the share of the Province of Samar for the same period. 

Henceforth, release the share of each barangay from RPT collections pursuant to Section 271(d) of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others concerned to implement, without any further delay, the programs/projects under the 20% Development Fund which have not yet been started.  Henceforth, ensure that the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be required to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommended that management indicate the basic information in the preparation of the Purchase Order, Request for Quotations and Purchase Request as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 and Republic Act 9184.

We recommended that management desist from charging to government funds the holding of the Search for Mutya Han Tarangnan 2018 and strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that management secure a comprehensive fire insurance for its properties with insurable interest so that the Municipality will be indemnified in case of loss or damage to its properties caused by natural and man-made calamities. Likewise, include in its budget the corresponding insurance premiums.

1.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to reclassify the fully
depreciated properties from Other Property, Plant and Equipment account to
the proper Property, Plant and Equipment accounts, and disclose the same in
the Notes to Financial Statements.

2.8 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others concerned to
implement, without any further delay, the programs/projects under the 20%
Development Fund which have not yet been started. Henceforth, ensure that
the 20% Development Fund is properly, promptly and fully utilized pursuant
to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

3.10 We recommended that the LCE require:
a) the LDRRMO to incorporate in the Annual LDRRMFIP the list of projects
and activities that should be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of
previous years; to regularly prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization
of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the
same to the Audit Team within the reglementary period;
b) the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries (RAOs) for
LDRRMF; and
c) the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for the LDRRMF
and ensure that the amounts disclosed in Notes to Financial Statements
reconcile with the amounts presented in the Report of Sources and
Utilization.
3.11 Henceforth, the LCE shall ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for
disaster risk reduction and management activities, and proper accounting and
reporting thereof is made pursuant to NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No.
2014-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

4.9 We recommended that the LCE be more judicious in authorizing employees to
attend seminars/trainings. In choosing whom to send to a particular training,
the LCE should consider the employee’s position/function, qualifications and
ability to impart knowledge acquired from the training, among others.

4.10 We also recommended that the Municipality refrain from conducting seminar-
workshops in venues outside of the geographical island where the Municipality
is located, particularly in expensive/luxury hotels, unless it can be shown that
the choice of venue is most advantageous to the government. Moreover,
management should exercise prudence in utilizing the limited funds of the
Municipality to avoid illegal, irregular, unnecessary, excessive, excessive,
extravagant and unconscionable expenditures, pursuant to the pertinent
provisions of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-003 and DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-59.

5.3 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to withhold the correct amount of taxes from all
honoraria payments and remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue
(BIR) within the reglementary period, pursuant to Sections 5 and 7 of RR No.
11-2018.

6.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
continue to correctly withhold the mandatory contributions and loan
amortizations of municipal officials and employees from their respective
salaries, and remit them promptly to GSIS, Pag-IBIG Fund and PhilHealth, as
mandated by RA No. 8291, RA No. 9679, and RA No. 7875 and their IRRs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant/Accounting Office to: (a) undertake corrective measures and prepare the necessary journal entries to rectify the deficiencies involving the stale checks still considered as outstanding checks; (b) prepare the necessary journal entries to record all reconciling items found to be valid; (c) consider the proper disposition/closure of the dormant bank account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 29, 2015; and (d) prepare and furnish the Audit Team with SLs for the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive file a request for write-off of the Due from Local Government Units account, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to: 1) record daily collections and deposits based on the RCDs; 2) balance all her cashbooks daily; and 3) maintain a cashbook for Cash Advance under the Trust Fund, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM).

We recommended the Municipal Government of Tarangnan provide immediately the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with a safe or vault for the safekeeping of accountable forms and undeposited collections and/or undisbursed cash advance. Likewise, provide the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with a separate office space which is adequately protected from intrusions from unauthorized persons.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others concerned to implement, without any further delay, all the programs/projects under the 20% Development Fund.
We also recommend that henceforth, management ensure that the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Not See similar findings in AAR No. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF using the format in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and its support groups strictly adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR, including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the conduct of a strict evaluation of bids and the submission of complete contract documents.
observations.managementAction: This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice/s of Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors concerned to post the required warranty securities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ascertain the exact amount of share of each barangay from the Municipality’s Real Property Tax collections for CYs 2015-2018, and release them to the barangays immediately.
Likewise, release the share of the Province of Samar for the same period.
Henceforth, release the share of each barangay from RPT collections pursuant to Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 48 of the General Provisions of the GAA of FY 2018 in the computation and disbursement of salaries and wages of agency officials and employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others concerned to implement, without any further delay, the programs/projects under the 20% Development Fund which have not yet been started.
Henceforth, ensure that the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that management consider the immediate closure or eventual phase out of the open dump site, and ensure that the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan be fully implemented pursuant to RA No. 9003 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer release to the Barangay Treasurers, on a quarterly basis, the barangay shares from RPT collections in order for the barangay to utilize this fund for their priority projects.

We recommended that the Local School Board manage and utilize its funds efficiently and effectively to ensure that the students of the public schools within the municipality could benefit from the PPAs of the Special Education Fund.
We also recommended the strict compliance of expenses chargeable to the Fund as provided for in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly implement the rules and regulations regarding the submission of all necessary documents required in EO 298 for all travel claims and to require all employees and officials to attach the electronic copy of official receipts instead of an RER for claims of taxi fare.

We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network.
Likewise, require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and its support groups strictly adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR, including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the conduct of a strict evaluation of bids and the submission of complete contract documents.

We recommended that the BAC procure goods and services thru competitive bidding and may resort to alternative method of procurement only after satisfying the conditions required thereon as provided for in the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Procurement documents should be properly accomplished to ascertain the legality, propriety and validity of procurement transactions.

We recommended that management implement the basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required as required under R.A. No. 9003 by involving closely the Barangay officials and civic leaders in the strict implementation of the existing environmental laws, and intensifying the information campaign at the household level on the ordinances issued by the Municipality. If possible seek the help of private companies/organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste and “no littering” policies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain and update property cards per category of PPE and the Municipal Accountant to also maintain and update PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) for land. Both officers should reconcile their records regularly.
Likewise, cause the conduct of physical inventory of all its properties and submit a report thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be required to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommended that management indicate the basic information in the preparation of the Purchase Order, Request for Quotations and Purchase Request as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 and Republic Act 9184.

We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) Secretariat be required to indicate in the APP the modes of procurement of goods and services and also to indicate in the PPMPs whether the PAPs will be contracted out or implemented by administration.
Likewise, require the Budget Office to furnish the BAC Secretariat a copy of the budget to enable the latter to determine whether the PPMPs prepared by the end-users conformed to their respective budgets to avoid frequent revision and/or preparation of supplemental PPMPs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require collecting clerks to turnover their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer and for the latter to remit her collections daily and intact to the depository bank.
Likewise, require collecting clerks to prepare a Report of Collection and Deposits daily to facilitate turnover of collections.

It is strongly recommended that the management should exert extra effort to trace its historical cost to its recorded valuation of Land, Land Improvements and Buildings and be reconciled with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor.
Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipality should be supported either with an original or transfer certificates of title registered in the name of the LGU.

We recommended that the BAC procure goods and services thru competitive contracting and may resort to alternative method of procurement only after satisfying the conditions required thereon as provided for in the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Procurement documents should be properly accomplished to ascertain the legality, propriety and validity of procurement transactions.

We recommended that management secure comprehensive fire insurance for its properties with insurable interest so that the Municipality will be indemnified in case of loss or damage to its properties caused by natural and man-made calamities. Likewise, include in its budget the corresponding insurance premiums.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be required to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the collecting clerks turnover their collections daily to Municipal Treasurer and for her to remit her collections daily and intact to the depository bank. Likewise, require collecting clerks to prepare a Report of Collection and Deposits daily to facilitate turnover of collections.

It is strongly recommended that management exert extra effort to trace its historical cost to its recorded valuation of Land, Land Improvements and Buildings and be reconciled with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor. Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipality should be supported either with an original or transfer certificates of title registered in the name of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the deficiency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to
post the subject warranty securities, where appropriate. This
recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice/s
of Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors concerned to post the required
warranty securities. Henceforth, the management should strictly enforce compliance with the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in the implementation of
infrastructure projects and procurement of goods, particularly on the posting of
warranty securities before final payment.

We recommended that management desist from charging to government
funds the holding of the Search for Mutya Han Tarangnan 2018 and strictly adhere to
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary
expenditures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ascertain the exact amount of share
of each barangay from the Municipality’s Real Property Tax collections for CYs
2015-2018, and release them to the barangays immediately. Likewise, release
the share of the Province of Samar for the same period. Henceforth, release the share of each barangay from RPT collections pursuant
to Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 48 of the General
Provisions of the GAA of FY 2018 in the computation and disbursement of
salaries and wages of agency officials and employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others
concerned to implement, without any further delay, the programs/projects
under the 20% Development Fund which have not yet been started, considering
that the issues and concerns that caused the delay were already resolved. Henceforth, ensure that the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully
utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that management consider the immediate closure or eventual
phase out of the open dump site, and ensure that the Municipality’s Solid Waste
Management Plan be fully implemented pursuant to RA No. 9003 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw adjusting entries to record the liquidation of cash advances for Confidential and Intelligence Expenses for CY 2010-2014 amounting to P6,000,000.00. Further, the Local Chief Executive should coordinate with the former Municipal Mayor to effect the immediate compliance with the AOMs issued by the ICFAU. Moreover, management should observe diligently the compliance with the aforementioned rules and regulations on the setting up, granting and liquidation of cash advances for Confidential and Intelligence Fund.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented Implemented Further, the Local Chief Executive should coordinate with the former Municipal Mayor to effect the immediate compliance with the AOMs issued by the ICFAU. Moreover, management should observe diligently the compliance with the aforementioned rules and regulations on the setting up, granting and liquidation of cash advances for Confidential and Intelligence Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer endeavor to produce the financial records needed that will constitute the correct basis for the computation and recording of the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables. Likewise, the municipality should regularly update the Real Property Tax Account Register/taxpayer’s index cards to facilitate the accurate computation and presentation of the RPT/SET receivables in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer release to the Barangay Treasurers, on a quarterly basis, the barangay shares from RPT collections in order for the barangay to utilize this fund for their priority projects.
observations.reason: A similar observation is incorporated in Finding No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the School Board manage and utilize its funds efficiently and effectively to ensure that the students of the public schools within the municipality could benefit from the PPAs of the Special Education Fund. We also recommended the strict compliance of expenses chargeable to the Fund as provided in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017.

We recommended that management limit the use of its 20% Development Funds for purposes that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. Likewise, require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure strict adherence to the provisions of the aforementioned Joint Memorandum Circular. Further, ensure that the 20% Development Fund be fully utilized to achieve optimum desirable socio-economic and environmental development.
observations.reason: A similar observation is incorporated in Finding No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exercise prudence in the use of government funds. Seminars and trainings should be conducted within the province to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other priority projects that would better benefit the municipality. Documentary requirements such as Activity Design, Post Activity Report and other necessary documents like approval/authority from DILG Regional Director if seminar/training is outside of Region VIII should be attached for those activities held outside the municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the rules and regulations regarding the submission of all necessary documents required in EO 298 for all travel claims and to require all employees and officials to attach the electronic copy of official receipts instead of an RER for claims of taxi fare.

We recommended that management procure telephone/communications services for cellular/mobile phones in accordance with Appendix 21 of the IRR of the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184). Likewise, officials and employees who are entitled to the Telephone Expenses allocation/budget should be in line with item No. 5, Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-003. Also, require that all claims for telephone/communication services under MOOE be supported with Statements of Account/Bill; official receipts/invoices and certification that calls are official as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001. Moreover, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a rate not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and beyond just measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication services providers. In addition, the municipality should submit a copy of existing memorandum or order and Sanggunian Bayan Resolution detailing the guidelines for all claims of the Telephone Expense and the corresponding rates entitled for each official or employee.

We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.
observations.reason: A similar observation is incorporated in Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the Municipal’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

We recommended that management implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under RA No. 9003, by involving closely the Barangay officials and civic leaders in the strict implementation of the existing environmental laws, and intensifying the information campaign at the household level on the proper segregation of waste and “no littering” policies. If possible seek the help of private companies/organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste and “no littering” policies.
observations.reason: A similar observation is incorporated in Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU in coordination with the Social Welfare Officer formulate policies/guidelines for approval by the Sanggunian on the grant of assistance to individual in crisis situation. We further recommend that the cash advances and its liquidation be supported by a summary of expenses and the screening documents, respectively, to be submitted to the audit team for validation purposes.

We recommended that management review and revise their job order contracts and start paying wages on a daily basis to conform to the guidelines provided in CSC Memorandum No. 17, s. 2002 promulgating CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 dated June 5, 2002.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant maintain and update property cards per category of PPE and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) for land. Both officers should reconcile their records regularly. Likewise, cause the conduct of physical inventory of all its properties and submit a report thereon.

We recommended that management indicate the basic information in the preparation of the Purchase Order, Request for Quotations and Purchase Request particularly the specifications of items to be purchased as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 and Republic Act 9184.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat be required to indicate in the APP the modes of procurement of goods and services and also to indicate in the PPMPs the information as to whether the PAPs will be contracted out or implemented by administration. Likewise, require the Budget Office to furnish the BAC Secretariat a copy of the budget to enable the latter to determine whether the PPMPs prepared by the end-users conformed to their respective budgets to avoid frequent revision and/or preparation of supplemental PPMPs.

We recommend that the Local Roads Asset Management guidelines on Local Road Network System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, to: (a) conduct physical count and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; (b) maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and (c) keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, contrary to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
