compliance with the AOMs issued by the ICFAU. Moreover, management should
observe diligently the compliance with the aforementioned rules and regulations on
the setting up, granting and liquidation of cash advances for Confidential and
Intelligence Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer endeavor to produce the financial
records needed that will constitute the correct basis for the computation and
recording of the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables. Likewise, the
municipality should regularly update the Real Property Tax Account
Register/taxpayer’s index cards to facilitate the accurate computation and
presentation of the RPT/SET receivables in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer release to the Barangay
Treasurers, on a quarterly basis, the barangay shares from RPT collections in order
for the barangay to utilize this fund for their priority projects.

We recommended that the Local School Board manage and utilize its funds
efficiently and effectively to ensure that the students of the public schools within the
municipality could benefit from the PPAs of the Special Education Fund. We also
recommend the strict compliance of expenses chargeable to the Fund as provided for
in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of
2017.

We recommended that management limit the use of its 20% Development
Funds for purposes that are outlined in DILG- DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
(JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. Likewise, require the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions of the same
Joint Memorandum Circular. Further, ensure that the 20% Development Fund be
fully utilized to achieve the optimum desirable socio-economic and environmental
development.

We recommended that management exercise prudence in the use of
government funds. Seminars and trainings should be conducted within the province
to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other
priority projects that would better benefit the municipality. Documentary
requirements such as Activity Design, Post Activity Report and other necessary
documents like approval/authority from DILG Regional Director for
seminars/trainings outside of Region VIII should be attached for those activities held
outside the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the rules and
regulations regarding the submission of all necessary documents required in EO 298
for all travel claims. Further, we recommended to require all employees and officials
to attach the electronic copy of official receipts instead of an RER for claims of taxi
fare.

We recommended that management procure telephone/communications
services for cellular/mobile phones in accordance with Appendix 21 of the IRR of the
Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184). Likewise, officials and employees
who are entitled to the Telephone Expenses allocation/budget should be in line with
item No. 5, Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-003.

Also, the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to
require that all claims for telephone/communication services under MOOE be
supported with Statements of Account/Bill; from telecommunication service
providers, official receipts/invoices and certification that calls are official as required
in COA Circular No. 2012-001. Likewise, official receipts/invoices printed in thermal
papers should be photocopied.

Moreover, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a rate
not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and beyond just
measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into account the
prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication services
providers.

In addition, we recommended that the municipality submit a copy of existing
memorandum or order and Sanggunian Bayan Resolution detailing the guidelines
for all claims of the Telephone Expense and the corresponding rates entitled for each
official or employee.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the reciprocal accounts and adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the Municipal’s financial operation as of a given period of time.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant reconciled the said accounts as effect shown in Consolidated Statement of Financial Position CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee and its support groups strictly adhere to the bidding processes prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR, including the dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the conduct of a strict / evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the submission of complete contract documents.
observations.reason: There were still lapses in the conduct of bidding processes prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR during contract review of infrastructure projects for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC procure goods and services thru competitive bidding and may resort to alternative method of procurement only after satisfying the required conditions thereon as provided for in the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Procurement documents should be properly accomplished to ascertain the legality, propriety and validity of procurement transactions.
observations.reason: There were still lapses in the compliance of the bidding process prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR during contract review of procurement documents for CY 2017. Some procurement documents were not properly filled with necessary data, thus transactions could not be reviewed or evaluated properly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board strictly observe the rules and regulations on the utilization of the Special Education Fund as provided in Section 99(a), 100 (c), and 272 of RA 7160, as well as DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s 1998 dated April 14, 1998.
observations.reason: There were still lapses; Project implementation is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implement the basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003, by closely involving Barangay officials and civic leaders in the strict implementation on the existing environmental ordinances, and intensifying the information campaign at the household level on the ordinances issued by the Municipality. If possible, seek the help of private companies/organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste and “no littering” policies.
observations.reason: There were still lapses in the implementation; intensify information campaign and seek private assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU in coordination with Social Welfare Officer formulate policies/guidelines on the grant of assistance to individuals in crisis situation and submit the same to the Sanggunian for adoption. Require that the cash advances and its liquidation be supported by a summary of expenses and the screening documents, respectively, to be submitted to the audit team for validation purposes.
observations.reason: AAR submitted policies/guidelines on the grant of financial assistance to individuals in crisis situation and was approved by the SB and Sanggunian for adoption. The cash advances and its liquidation were supported by a summary of expenses and the respective documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management review and revise their job order contracts and start paying wages on a daily basis to conform to the guidelines provided in CSC Memorandum No. 17, s. 2002 promulgating CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 dated June 5, 2002.
observations.reason: The recommendation was implemented in the 3rd quarter of CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management secure a comprehensive fire insurance for its properties with insurable interest so that the Municipality will be indemnified in case of loss or damage to its properties caused by natural and man-made calamities. Likewise, include in the budget the corresponding insurance premiums.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, management did not appropriate budget/ allocation for insurance of assets due to constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish a GAD Focal Point System and ensure its functionality. Likewise, prepare GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and submit the same to DILG Regional Office for review and approval. Moreover, see to it that the details or breakdown of the specific programs, projects and activities of each office are clearly identified in the obligation requests, obligation vouchers and supporting documents to ensure that the expenditures are aligned with the GAD Plan.
observations.reason: Implemented. Moreover, ensure that details of specific programs/activities are stated in obligation documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Due to the Geographical Location of the municipality management had a hard time to submit the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to stamp the disbursement vouchers and all their supporting documents “PAID” upon payment and indicate the number of the check issued and the date of actual payment to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in payment of future claims.

We recommend that management indicate the basic information in the preparation of the Purchase Order, Request for Quotations and Purchase Request as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 and Republic Act 9184.
observations.reason: Some submitted Purchase Orders, Request for Quotations and Purchase Requests lacked basic data or information.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC Secretariat be required to indicate in the APP the modes of procurement of goods and services and also to indicate in the PPMPs whether the programs, activities and projects (PAPs) will be contracted out or implemented by administration.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat now indicates procurement modes and the PPMPs were updated to avoid frequent revision.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor require collecting clerks to turnover their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer and for the latter to remit her collections daily and intact to the depository bank. Likewise, require collecting clerks to prepare a Report of Collection and Deposits daily.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR Finding No. 3; AAR 2013 Finding No. 2
observations.reason: Continued failure of the Liquidating Officer to direct all revenue collectors to remit collections regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to submit (a) duly approved and properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets which should be serially numbered, a summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels, and (b) Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation and equipment. Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 in order to control and regulate the use of government vehicles.

We recommend that the accounting and reporting guidelines on Local Roads
Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to
maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network.
Likewise, require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory
and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads and its components. Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the
physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network
System.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal
accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present
reliable data on the Municipal’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee and its support groups
strictly adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR,
including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the conduct of
a strict evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the submission of
complete contract documents.

We recommend that the BAC procure goods and services thru competitive
bidding and may resort to alternative method of procurement only after satisfying the
conditions required thereon as provided for in the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
Procurement documents should be properly accomplished to ascertain the legality,
propriety and validity of procurement transactions.

We recommend that management limit the use the 20% Development Funds
for purposes that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

Management should clearly identify the proposed programs and projects of the
National Government Agencies requiring counterpart and the purpose to which the
counterpart will be applied. If the projects will eventually increases the assets of the
LGU, then this will qualify to be funded from 20% Development Fund, but if not, then
this will be charged to the MOOE.

We recommend that the Local School Board strictly observe the rules and
regulations on the utilization of the Special Education Fund as provided for in
Section 99(a), 100 (c), and 272 of RA 7160, as well as DECS, DBM and DILG Joint
Circular No. 01 s 1998 dated April 14, 1998.

We recommend that management implement the basic environmental laws
on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003, by
involving closely the Barangay officials and civic leaders in the strict
implementation on the existing environmental laws, and intensifying the
information campaign at the household level on the ordinances issued by the
Municipality. If possible seek the help of private companies/organizations in the
conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper
segregation of waste and “no littering” policies.

We recommend that the LGU in coordination with Social Welfare Officer
formulate policies/guidelines on the grant of financial assistance to individuals in
crisis situation and submit the same to the Sanggunian for adoption. We further
recommend that the cash advances and its liquidation, be supported by a summary
of expenses and the screening documents, respectively, to be submitted to the audit
team for validation purposes.

We recommend that management review and revise their job order contracts
and start paying wages on a daily basis to conform to the guidelines provided in CSC
Memorandum No. 17, s. 2002 promulgating CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 dated
June 5, 2002.
